Existing Agreement Limits for Operation Warp Speed, , , which provided that the value of any modification to, or order made under, a contract or other agreement by the Department of Defense on or after , to address the COVID–19 pandemic through vaccines and other therapeutic measures generally would not be counted toward any limit established prior to , on the total estimated amount of all projects to be issued under the contract or other agreement, was repealed by , , .Pub. L. 117–263, div. A, title VIII, § 883136 Stat. 2746Pub. L. 119–60, div. A, title VIII, § 811(b)(1)139 Stat. 948Dec. 23, 2022March 1, 2020March 1, 2020Dec. 18, 2025

Relaxation of Undefinitized Contract Action Limitations and Restrictions Related to National Emergency for Coronavirus Disease 2019, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–136, div. B, title III, § 13004134 Stat. 522Mar. 27, 2020“(a), United States Code [now ], shall not apply to any undefinitized contract action of the Department of Defense related to the national emergency for the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID–19).Section 2326(b)(3) of title 1010 U.S.C. 3372(a)(2)(B)“(b)In this section, the term ‘undefinitized contract action’ has the meaning given that term in , United States Code [probably means , now , which defines “undefinitized contractual action”].”section 2326(j)(6) of title 10section 2326(j)(1) of title 1010 U.S.C. 3377(b)(1), , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–136, div. B, title III, § 13005134 Stat. 522Mar. 27, 2020“(a)The head of an agency may waive the provisions of , United States Code [now ], with respect to a contract of such agency if the head of the agency determines that the waiver is necessary due to the national emergency for the Coronavirus Disease 2019 (COVID–19).section 2326(b) of title 1010 U.S.C. 3372(a)“(b)In this section, the term ‘head of an agency’ has the meaning given that term in section 2302(2) [probably means section 2302(1); now ] of title 10, United States Code.”10 U.S.C. 3004

children.heading: Contractual Action to Provide Time for Definitization of Contractual Terms, Specifications, and Price; Limitations on Obligation of Funds.—
children.label: a Contractual Action to Provide Time for Definitization of Contractual Terms, Specifications, and Price; Limitations on Obligation of Funds.—

children.heading: .—Terms for time for definitization to be included in contractual action
children.label: 1 .—Terms for time for definitization to be included in contractual action
A
children.text: the end of the 180-day period beginning on the date on which the contractor submits a qualifying proposal to definitize the contractual terms, specifications, and price; or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which the amount of funds obligated under the contractual action is equal to more than 50 percent of the negotiated overall ceiling price for the contractual action.

children.heading: Limitation on obligation of funds before definitization.—
children.label: 2 Limitation on obligation of funds before definitization.—

children.heading: .—50 percent limitation
children.label: A .—50 percent limitation
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), the contracting officer for an undefinitized contractual action may not obligate with respect to such contractual action an amount that is equal to more than 50 percent of the negotiated overall ceiling price until the contractual terms, specifications, and price are definitized for such contractual action.

children.heading: .—75 percent limitation when contractor submits qualifying proposal
children.label: B .—75 percent limitation when contractor submits qualifying proposal
children.text: If a contractor submits a qualifying proposal (as defined in ) to definitize an undefinitized contractual action before an amount equal to more than 50 percent of the negotiated overall ceiling price is obligated on such action, the contracting officer for such action may not obligate with respect to such contractual action an amount that is equal to more than 75 percent of the negotiated overall ceiling price until the contractual terms, specifications, and price are definitized for such contractual action.section 3377(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Inapplicability with respect to purchase of initial spares
children.label: 4 .—Inapplicability with respect to purchase of initial spares
children.text: This subsection does not apply to an undefinitized contractual action for the purchase of initial spares.

children.heading: Limitation on Unilateral Definitization by Contracting Officer.—
children.label: b Limitation on Unilateral Definitization by Contracting Officer.—
A
children.text: the service acquisition executive for the military department that awarded the contract, or the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment if the contract was awarded by a Defense Agency or other component of the Department of Defense, approves the definitization in writing;

children.label: B
children.text: the contracting officer provides a copy of the written approval to the contractor; and

children.label: C
children.text: a period of 30 calendar days has elapsed after the written approval is provided to the contractor.

children.heading: .—Treatment of unilateral definitization of a contract as a final decision
children.label: 2 .—Treatment of unilateral definitization of a contract as a final decision
children.text: A unilateral definitization by a contracting officer shall be considered a final decision under chapter 71 of title 41, and a contractor may appeal this decision to the Armed Services Board of Contract Appeals or the United States Court of Federal Claims.

children.heading: 180.—-day requirement
children.label: 1 180.—-day requirement
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), a contracting officer of the Department of Defense may not enter into an undefinitized contractual action for a foreign military sale unless the contractual action provides for agreement upon contractual terms, specifications, and price by the end of the 180-day period described in subsection (a)(1)(A).

children.heading: .—Waiver authority
children.label: 2 .—Waiver authority
children.text: The requirement under paragraph (1) may be waived in accordance with subsection (a)(3).
